helpful: The provisioned docket snapshot exposed the unusually long relist history.
CourtListener opinion access supplied the lower-court reasoning and the intervening Wolford decision.
The committed statpack provided paid-petition and relist base rates.
gaps: No petition, questions-presented, brief-in-opposition, or documents manifest was provisioned.
The local corpus query service was unreachable, so similar case-level priors could not be retrieved.
CourtListener opinion searches did not expose the current SCOTUS petition filings.
notes: The forecast remained feasible from the snapshot, CourtListener's pre-disposition legal materials, and committed base rates. The missing filing text materially reduced confidence in the precise vehicle analysis.
